info.title: Google Developer Group on Campus, UNN Community API

get.description:
Build the Google consent URL with a signed state token and redirect.
The state payload includes a random nonce and a User-Agent hash for
context binding — preventing cross-device replay of the state token.

Google redirects here with ?state=...&code=...
We verify the signed state (signature + expiry + UA binding), exchange
the code for tokens, find-or-create the user, and redirect to frontend.

parameters.name: from_date

schema.description: Filter events on or after this date
schema.title: From Date

parameters.description: Filter events on or after this date

get.description: Get all events the current user is registered for.

schema.title: Response My Registrations Endpoint Events Me Registrations Get

post.description: Add a speaker to an event. Admin only.

patch.description: Update a speaker on an event. Admin only.

delete.description: Remove a speaker from an event. Admin only.

post.description: Register the current user for an event.

delete.description: Unregister the current user from an event.

get.description: Check if the current user is registered for an event.

schema.title: Response List Users Admin Users  Get

patch.description: Update current user's profile (full_name, phone, bio).

post.description:
Create a new project.

- **Personal projects**: Can be created by regular users
- **Community projects**: Can only be created by admins

The project_type in the payload determines the type of project.
Authorization is automatically enforced based on the user's role.

get.description:
Fetch all projects with optional filtering and pagination.

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
- Supports filtering by project type (personal/community) and status (ongoing/completed)
- Includes creator and contributors in the response

schema.title: Response Get All Projects Endpoint Api V1 Projects  Get

get.description: Get all project applications for the current user.

schema.title: Response My Applications Endpoint Api V1 Projects Me Applications Get

get.description:
Fetch a single project by its ID.

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
- Includes creator and contributors in the response
- Returns 404 if project not found

patch.description:
Update an existing project.

- **Personal projects**: Only the creator can update
- **Community projects**: Only admins can update

All fields are optional - only provided fields will be updated.
Note: project_type and creator_id cannot be changed.

delete.description:
Delete a project.

- **Personal projects**: Only the creator can delete
- **Community projects**: Only admins can delete

Deleting a project will also cascade delete all contributors.

post.description:
Add a contributor to a project.

- Only the project creator/owner can add contributors
- The user being added must exist in the database
- Duplicate contributors will return a 400 error

get.description:
Get all contributors for a project.

- Public endpoint (no authentication required)
- Returns list of contributors with user details

schema.title: Response Get Contributors Endpoint Api V1 Projects  Project Id  Contributors Get

delete.description:
Remove a contributor from a project.

- Only the project creator/owner can remove contributors
- Returns 404 if the contributor is not found

parameters.name: user_id

schema.description: User ID of the contributor to remove
schema.title: User Id

parameters.description: User ID of the contributor to remove

post.description: Apply to contribute to a project.

get.description: List all applications for a project. Project owner or admin only.

schema.title: Response List Applications Endpoint Api V1 Projects  Project Id  Applications Get

patch.description: Approve a project application and add the user as a contributor.

delete.description: Reject an application. Project owner or admin only.

post.description:
Submit a new blog post.

- Available to all authenticated users (including admins).
- **Admin posts are auto-approved** and immediately publicly visible.
- Regular user posts are created with **status = pending**.

get.description:
List all approved blog posts.

- Public endpoint — no authentication required.
- Ordered by approval date (newest first).

schema.title: Response List Approved Blogposts Api V1 Blogposts  Get

get.description:
Get the current user's own blog posts (all statuses).

- Requires authentication.
- Returns pending, approved, and rejected posts for the author.

schema.title: Response Get My Blogposts Api V1 Blogposts Me Get

post.description:
Toggle like on a blog post.

- Requires authentication.
- Only allowed on **approved** posts.
- If already liked → unliked. If not liked → liked.

Returns `{ "liked": bool, "likes_count": int }`.

Add a comment to a blog post.

- Requires authentication.
- Only allowed on **approved** posts.

get.description:
Get comments for a blog post.

- Public endpoint — no authentication required.
- Ordered by `created_at DESC`.
- Paginated via `skip` / `limit`.

schema.title: Response List Comments Api V1 Blogposts  Post Id  Comments Get

get.description:
Fetch a single blog post by ID.

- **Approved** → publicly accessible (no auth required).
- **Pending / Rejected** → only the author or an admin can view.

schema.title: Response Get Blogpost Api V1 Blogposts  Post Id  Get

get.description:
Admin: list all blog posts, optionally filtered by status.

Use `?status=pending` to see the moderation queue. Omit status or use status=all for all posts.
Returns posts with author (user) loaded for display.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status: pending, approved, rejected, or all
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status: pending, approved, rejected, or all

schema.title: Response Admin List Blogposts Api V1 Admin Blogposts  Get

patch.description:
Approve a pending blog post.

- Only admins can approve.
- An admin **cannot** approve their own post.
- Only `pending` posts can be approved.

Reject a pending blog post.

- Only admins can reject.
- Only `pending` posts can be rejected.
- An optional `rejection_reason` can be provided.

Update a comment's content.

- Requires authentication.
- Only the **author** can edit their own comment.

delete.description:
Delete a comment.

- Requires authentication.
- The **author** can delete their own comment.
- An **admin** can delete any comment.

get.description:
List non-admin community members. Available to any authenticated user.
Paginated via skip and limit.

schema.title: Response List Community Members Api V1 Community Members Get

get.description: List team members ordered by display_order. Used by landing page builders section.

schema.title: Response List Team Api V1 Team Get

post.description: Authenticated endpoint to upload media to Cloudinary.

ApplicantRead.title: ApplicantRead
ApplicantRead.description:
Schema for reading applicant data from the API.

Used when retrieving application information, including approval status.

Attributes:
    id (UUID): Unique application identifier
    user_id (UUID): ID of the user who applied
    project_id (UUID): ID of the project applied to
    role (str): Desired role in the project
    is_contributor (bool): Whether the application has been approved

Notes:
    - is_contributor=False means pending approval
    - is_contributor=True means approved and can contribute

BlogPostAdminRead.title: BlogPostAdminRead
BlogPostAdminRead.description: Full blog post response for admins — includes moderation details.

BlogPostCreate.title: BlogPostCreate
BlogPostCreate.description: Payload for submitting a new blog post (community members).

BlogPostRead.title: BlogPostRead
BlogPostRead.description: Public-facing blog post response (approved posts only).

ProjectContributorCreate.title: ProjectContributorCreate
ProjectContributorCreate.description:
Schema for adding a contributor to a project.

Attributes:
    user_id (UUID): ID of the user to add as contributor
    role (str): Contributor's role in the project

ProjectContributorRead.title: ProjectContributorRead
ProjectContributorRead.description:
Schema for reading contributor data.

Attributes:
    id (UUID): Contributor record identifier
    user_id (UUID): User ID of the contributor
    role (str): Contributor's role
    added_at (datetime): When they were added
    user (UserBasic): Nested user details

ProjectCreate.title: ProjectCreate
ProjectCreate.description:
Schema for creating a new project.

This schema defines the fields required when creating a new collaborative
project in the GDGoC UNN community.

Attributes:
    project_type (ProjectType): Type of project (personal or community)
    title (str): Project name
    description (str): Detailed project description and goals
    duration (str, optional): Expected project duration (e.g., "3 months")
    start_date (date, optional): Project start date
    end_date (date, optional): Project completion/deadline date
    github_repo (str, optional): GitHub repository URL
    demo_video_url (str, optional): URL to project demo or presentation

ProjectDetailRead.title: ProjectDetailRead
ProjectDetailRead.description:
Detailed project schema with creator and contributors.

Extends ProjectRead with relational data.

Attributes:
    creator (UserBasic): Project creator details
    contributors (list[ProjectContributorRead]): List of contributors

ProjectStatus.title: ProjectStatus
ProjectStatus.description:
Enumeration of possible project statuses.

Attributes:
    ongoing: Project is currently active and accepting contributions
    completed: Project has been finished

ProjectType.title: ProjectType
ProjectType.description:
Enumeration of project types.

Attributes:
    personal: Project created by a regular community member
    community: Official community project created by an admin

ProjectUpdate.title: ProjectUpdate
ProjectUpdate.description:
Schema for updating an existing project.

All fields are optional to allow partial updates.
Note: project_type and creator_id cannot be updated.

UserBasic.title: UserBasic
UserBasic.description: Shared basic user info for nested responses.
